description_long: This house features a simple rectangular structure with a prominent gable roof covered in reddish-brown tiles. The roof extends outward, creating deep eaves on all sides, and includes a central brick chimney. The front facade is marked by a triangular gable overhang above the entryway, with contrasting black and white trim. The exterior walls are finished in a light gray material, likely stucco or painted brick, with a dark horizontal band running along the base. Windows are minimal and symmetrically placed, with a large window visible on each gable end. The overall design is modest and functional, typical of suburban or small-town residential neighborhoods.
description: A single-story house with a reddish-brown gable roof, central chimney, and simple gray exterior walls with black trim. The design is modest, with deep eaves and minimal, symmetrically placed windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main gable overhang, central chimney, and black-trimmed entryway.
description_view_1: Side gable view highlighting the deep roof eaves and a large window beneath the roof peak.
description_view_2: Rear view with a plain roofline, visible chimney, and minimal exterior detailing.
description_view_3: Opposite side gable view, mirroring the other side with a large window and extended eaves.
attribute_entryway_design: Triangular gable overhang above the main door, with black trim and no visible porch or steps.
